decomposition_notes: Goals 2-7 of the review-queue-code-diffs initiative: per-plugin diff-capture work. Each plugin lives in its own repo (except `stride` which is in this kanban repo at stride/) and ships independently. ALL six plugin goals depend on the contract published in G148/W719 (the per-file diff JSON contract reference doc). Each goal's first implementation task uses an identifier-dependency on W719; subsequent in-goal tasks depend on indices. The six plugin goals are INDEPENDENT of each other — backward compatibility means older plugin versions that haven't shipped capture continue to produce working reviews (no diff panel for those tasks). NO coordinated cutover is required. Release model follows the kanban-marketplace convention: bump plugin repo + bump stride-marketplace pin in the same release cycle (per memory: feedback_stride_marketplace_release.md). The `stride` plugin goal has an extra task (W*-skill-docs) because the canonical completion-payload skill body lives there and must document the new diff field for downstream agent operators.

goals.title: Capture per-file diffs in stride plugin (reference implementation)
goals.why: The `stride` plugin is the reference workflow plugin distributed via stride-marketplace and used by Claude Code agents. Without diff capture wired into its completion-payload assembly, the kanban-app review queue panel (G148) has nothing to render for tasks completed by stride-plugin agents — which is the majority of traffic. As the reference plugin, this work also serves as the implementation template the other five variants follow.
goals.what: Add a step (during after_doing hook execution or during completion-payload assembly — confirm during implementation by reading the existing stride-completing-tasks skill body) that runs `git diff` per changed file relative to the task base, truncates to 500 lines with the marker from the G148/W719 contract, and writes the unified-patch text into the per-file `diff` field of the completion payload. Update the stride-completing-tasks skill body to document the new field so downstream agent operators know about it. Add tests covering: (a) capture happy path, (b) 500-line truncation marker, (c) backward compatibility — legacy completion payload without diff field still validates server-side.
goals.where_context: stride/hooks/ (if capture happens at hook time), stride/skills/stride-completing-tasks/ (the API contract skill that documents the completion payload shape), and possibly stride/agents/task-reviewer.md (if review-time emission is the chosen capture point)
goals.description: Reference implementation of per-file diff capture. The other five plugin variants (stride-copilot, stride-gemini, stride-codex, stride-opencode, stride-pi) follow this implementation as a template. Backward compatibility is non-negotiable: tasks completed without diff data must continue to validate on the kanban-app side (per G148/W720).
goals.acceptance_criteria:
stride plugin captures per-file diffs during completion-payload assembly
Diffs are truncated to 500 lines per file with the marker from the G148/W719 contract
The stride-completing-tasks skill body documents the new diff field
Tasks completed without diff capture (e.g., older plugin versions in mixed environments) still validate server-side
New minor version released; stride-marketplace pin updated
No regressions in existing stride-plugin smoke tests
pitfalls: Do not implement capture before G148/W719 publishes the contract — the truncation marker and field name come from there
Do not skip the skill-body docs update — downstream agent operators consuming the stride plugin need to know about the new field
Do not bump the plugin without also bumping stride-marketplace pin in the same release cycle (per memory)
Do not break backward compatibility — older agents must keep producing valid completions

tasks.title: Implement per-file diff capture in stride plugin per G148/W719 contract
tasks.description: Add a capture step during the stride plugin's completion-payload assembly (or after_doing hook) that runs `git diff <base>..HEAD` per changed file, truncates to 500 lines per file with the marker from the G148/W719 contract, and writes the unified-patch text into the per-file `diff` field of the completion payload.
tasks.why: Without this, the kanban-app review-queue diff panel (G148) has no data to render for tasks completed by the stride plugin.
tasks.what: Choose capture point (during after_doing vs. during completion-payload assembly — read stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md to pick the location). Run `git diff` per file in the changed-files list. Apply 500-line truncation with the contract's marker convention. Encode as a string and attach to each per-file entry. Handle binary files by emitting the contract's binary placeholder rather than attempting to patch them.
tasks.where_context: stride/hooks/stride-hook.sh (if hook-time capture), stride/skills/stride-completing-tasks/ (if skill-driven assembly)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
On task completion, the stride plugin captures `git diff` per changed file relative to the task base
Diffs over 500 lines are truncated with the marker from G148/W719
Binary files emit the contract's binary placeholder, not a patch attempt
The captured payload is attached to the per-file entry as a `diff` string field
Legacy code path that doesn't run capture (e.g., when contract field is absent in skill version) still works
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Read G148/W719's published contract doc as the single source of truth for field name, truncation marker, binary convention
pitfalls: Do not start before W719 lands — the contract doc is the source of truth for marker / field-name conventions
Do not capture untracked or excluded files — `git diff` against the task base is the authoritative file set
Do not blow up on a repo with no `<base>` ref — fall back to capturing against HEAD~1 or document the limitation

verification_steps.expected_result: Hook tests pass including any new diff-capture cases

verification_steps.step_text: Run a small task through the stride plugin and inspect the POST /complete payload — confirm the per-file entries carry a `diff` string for non-binary files
verification_steps.expected_result: diff field present and well-formed for text files; binary placeholder for binary files

unit_tests: Truncation: diff over 500 lines is truncated with marker
Truncation: diff at exactly 500 lines is not truncated
Binary file: placeholder is emitted, no patch attempt
integration_tests: End-to-end completion of a small task produces a payload with diff data
manual_tests: Run against a real task in this repo and inspect the payload
edge_cases: File with very long single line

tasks.title: Document the per-file diff field in stride-completing-tasks skill body
tasks.description: Update the stride-completing-tasks skill body to document the new optional per-file `diff` field on the completion payload. Cross-link to the G148/W719 contract reference doc. The skill is the canonical reference for downstream agent operators implementing custom completion flows.
tasks.why: Agent operators reading the stride-completing-tasks skill expect a complete picture of the completion-payload shape. Without this update, the diff field is invisible to anyone writing a custom plugin against the stride API.
tasks.what: Add a section (or extend the existing payload-shape section) in stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md describing the per-file `diff` field — encoding, truncation, binary convention. Cross-link to the contract reference doc in the kanban repo. Note that the field is optional and backward compatibility is guaranteed.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Skill body documents the per-file diff field with the encoding, truncation, and binary conventions
Cross-link to G148/W719's contract reference doc is present
Backward-compatibility note is explicit (legacy completions without diff are still valid)
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Match existing skill-body voice — direct, names the actual mechanism, no marketing
Keep the addition minimal — the contract doc is the source of truth; the skill summarizes and links
pitfalls: Do not duplicate the contract doc — link to it; keep the skill body concise
Do not document fields that aren't actually emitted by the stride plugin yet — wait for task 0 to land first

verification_steps.expected_result: References to diff field, truncation, and binary convention appear

manual_tests: Read updated skill body end-to-end and confirm a new agent operator could implement capture from the skill alone

tasks.title: Add tests for stride-plugin diff capture: happy path, truncation, backward compatibility
tasks.description: Add tests exercising the new capture path: a normal completion produces diff data; a >500-line diff is truncated with the marker; a binary file gets the placeholder; a legacy completion without diff capture still produces a valid payload. Use the existing test harness in stride/hooks/test-*.sh as the pattern.
tasks.why: Without explicit tests, the capture step is a silent failure surface — `git diff` errors or path-resolution bugs would only surface when reviewers complain about missing diffs.
tasks.what: Extend stride/hooks/test-stride-hook.sh (or add a sibling test file) with cases for capture-happy, truncation-applied, binary-placeholder, and legacy-no-capture. Use small fixture diffs to keep test runtime short.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Tests cover capture happy path, truncation marker, binary placeholder, and legacy-no-capture
All new tests pass
No regression in existing hook tests
pitfalls: Do not write tests that depend on a specific git history — use fixtures or temp repos
Do not skip the legacy-no-capture case — it's the backward-compatibility guarantee

testing_strategy.coverage_target: All capture branches plus the legacy bypass

tasks.title: Release stride plugin and update stride-marketplace pin
tasks.description: Cut a versioned release of the stride plugin containing the diff-capture changes. Bump version (semver minor — adds new optional field to completion payload). Update CHANGELOG. Bump stride-marketplace pin in the same release cycle.
tasks.why: Per memory (feedback_stride_marketplace_release.md), Stride plugin releases require bumping BOTH the plugin repo AND stride-marketplace. Without the marketplace bump, installs continue to pull the old version.
tasks.what: Bump stride/.claude-plugin/plugin.json version. Add CHANGELOG entry. Tag the release. Bump stride-marketplace pin and commit + push. Verify install picks up the new version.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
stride plugin version bumped (semver minor)
CHANGELOG entry describes the diff-capture release
stride-marketplace pin updated and pushed
Fresh install picks up the new version
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Same release model as the G147/W718 stride-ideation release task — bump both repos in the same cycle
pitfalls: Do not bump only the plugin repo — marketplace pin must be updated in the same release cycle
Do not use `gh release create` from Claude — classifier-gated; surface the command for the user to run interactively
Do not skip the CHANGELOG entry

manual_tests: Fresh install and exercise a small task end-to-end with diff capture

goals.title: Capture per-file diffs in stride-copilot plugin
goals.why: stride-copilot agents complete tasks via Copilot CLI's tool surface and currently emit completion payloads without per-file diff data. Without parity with the stride reference plugin, Copilot-completed tasks render an empty diff panel in the kanban-app review queue.
goals.what: Implement per-file diff capture in the stride-copilot plugin conforming to the G148/W719 contract — capture, 500-line truncation with marker, binary placeholder. Add tests. Release with marketplace pin bump.
goals.where_context: stride-copilot plugin repo (separate from this kanban repo)
goals.description: Variant implementation following the stride-plugin reference (G149 / W725-W728). Independent of the other plugin variants — can ship in any order after G148/W719 contract is published.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
stride-copilot captures per-file diffs per the G148/W719 contract
Truncation marker and binary placeholder match the contract
Backward compatibility preserved
New minor release with marketplace pin updated
pitfalls: Do not start before W719 lands
Do not bump the plugin without bumping the marketplace pin

tasks.title: Implement per-file diff capture in stride-copilot per G148/W719 contract
tasks.description: Add per-file diff capture adapted to Copilot CLI's tool surface. Capture `git diff` per changed file, truncate to 500 lines with the marker, emit binary placeholder for binary files, attach to per-file entry of the completion payload.
tasks.why: Without this, stride-copilot-completed tasks have no diff data in the kanban-app review queue.
tasks.what: Locate the Copilot-CLI equivalent of stride's after_doing hook / completion-assembly step. Run `git diff` per file, apply truncation + marker, attach as `diff` field. Match the field name, marker, and binary convention from G148/W719.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Capture step runs during completion-payload assembly in stride-copilot
Field name, truncation marker, and binary convention match G148/W719
Backward-compatible — older agents still produce valid completions
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Mirror the stride-plugin reference implementation (G149 task 0) — same field name and conventions, adapted to Copilot's tool surface
pitfalls: Do not diverge from the contract — field name and marker must match across variants for kanban-app parsing

verification_steps.step_text: Run a task through stride-copilot and inspect the POST /complete payload
verification_steps.expected_result: diff field present per the contract

tasks.why: Tests prevent silent regressions in the capture step.
tasks.what: Add tests following stride-copilot's existing test harness pattern. Cover the four cases above using fixture diffs.
